title: settings.restore-defaults
description: Restore one, several, or all v1 settings keys to their in-code defaults by writing the literal default value as an explicit row (A3). Empty 'keys' restores all keys. Keys already at default return status='noop' with no row write and no audit event (R-3.1, cross-ref spec 002 noop pattern).

keys.description: List of v1 settings keys to restore. Empty or omitted means restore every key.

status.description: 'success' when at least one key was restored (literal default value written). 'noop' when every requested key was already at its default value — no row writes, no audit events (R-3.1).

restored.description: Keys for which the literal default value was written. Empty when status='noop'.

already_at_default.description: Keys that were already at their default value and were not written (R-3.1).

errors.message: One or more supplied keys are not part of the v1 schema. No keys are restored when the request contains any unknown key.
